findings.finding: The completion rule of scoring at least 8/10 with at least 1 point per response allows a learner to pass and be credited with outcome mastery without satisfying the explicit requirements of the course objectives. For example, a learner scoring 3/4 on the journey item (earning 0/1 on criterion-journey-links due to not explaining preparation links), 3/3 on relevance, and 3/3 on capabilities will pass the check with 9/10. However, they have failed the core requirement of objective-describe-subject-journey. Additionally, other 8/10 passing vectors allow failing critical criteria like safety and completion boundaries (criterion-capability-boundary).
findings.resolution: Align the completion logic in both assessment.json and content/04-finish.md to ensure that a learner cannot pass without satisfying the key rubric criteria mapped to the lesson's learning objectives. This can be achieved by: (1) raising the passing threshold or adding explicit 'must-pass' hurdle criteria in the grading engine for key rubric items (such as criterion-journey-links and criterion-capability-boundary); or (2) restructuring the assessment items/rubrics so that any mathematically passing score guarantees that the criteria of all mapped objectives are satisfied.

findings.finding: The tube flow model worked example provides a scientifically accurate representation of a fair test comparison. The average results (20 mL vs ~80 mL) and the ratios (~4x) clearly stand out above the small run-to-run variation (19-21 mL for Tube A, 78-82 mL for Tube B), demonstrating robust data interpretation within model limits.

limitations: Review is limited to the academic accuracy and curriculum alignment of this introductory welcome lesson.
No clinical evaluation is performed as this is a non-clinical, pre-medical introductory overview.
No verification of external materials beyond the provided syllabus source commit was performed.
